{"id":"bd-rgnx","title":"Codebase Modernization: Registry Refactor & Architecture Cleanup","description":"## Overview
Based on deep codebase review by external AI (Gemini 3 Pro), this epic tracks the transformation from a 'half-migrated' state to a fully modular plugin architecture.

## Current State (Problems)
1. **DriverType God Enum**: 300+ line match statement in registry.rs:1586 violates Open/Closed principle
2. **Dual Instantiation Paths**: Both factory-based (Line 1025) AND enum-based (Line 1586) coexist
3. **Conflicting Trait Definitions**: core.rs:665 vs capabilities.rs define competing Camera/Instrument traits
4. **UI Coupling in Core**: DeviceCategory::icon() puts emojis in hardware layer (capabilities.rs:138)
5. **Hardcoded Lab Config**: IP addresses and serial ports in library code (registry.rs:2350)
6. **ConfiguredDriver Enum**: factory.rs:67 defeats plugin architecture purpose

## Target State
- Purely string-based factory lookup in registry
- Single source of truth for capability traits (capabilities.rs)
- Generic UI panels generated from Parameterized metadata
- No hardcoded configuration in library code
- True plugin architecture where adding drivers requires no core changes

## Success Metrics
- Delete ~1000 lines from registry.rs
- Delete DriverType enum entirely
- UI crate size reduced by removing device-specific panels
- Adding new driver requires only: new crate + factory registration

## Phases
1. Core Architecture Cleanup (Immediate)
2. Reliability & Safety (Short Term)
3. UI Scalability (Medium Term)
4. Performance Optimization (Long Term)

## References
- Review source: Gemini 3 Pro deep analysis (2026-01-30)
- Key files: registry.rs, capabilities.rs, core.rs, factory.rs, driver.rs","notes":"2026-02-08: Gemini g3-pro deep review. {"id":"bd-izdj.13","title":"[P3] Release Build Optimization","description":"**Problem**: No LTO, default codegen-units. Missing easy performance gains.

**Root Cause**: Cargo.toml doesn't specify release optimizations.

**Files to modify**:
- `Cargo.toml` (workspace root)

**Implementation**:
Add to workspace Cargo.toml:
```toml
[profile.release]
lto = "thin"
codegen-units = 1
strip = true

[profile.release-daemon]
inherits = "release"
panic = "abort"  # Smaller binary, daemon doesn't need unwinding

[profile.release-gui]
inherits = "release"
panic = "unwind"  # GUI may benefit from panic recovery
```

**Expected Gains**:
- LTO: 3-5% runtime performance
- codegen-units=1: Better optimization, longer compile
- strip: ~30% smaller binaries
- panic=abort: ~5% smaller daemon binary

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Release profile updated
- [ ] `cargo build --release` uses new settings
- [ ] Binary size compared before/after
- [ ] Basic benchmark compared before/after
- [ ] CI updated if needed

**Effort**: 30 minutes (quick win)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:29:46Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-02-19T20:18:29Z","defer_until":"2026-06-01T00:00:00Z"}

{"id":"bd-izdj.14","title":"[P3] Plugin Versioning for DriverFactory","description":"**Problem**: No API version in `DriverFactory`; incompatible plugins fail silently.

**Root Cause**: Trait has no versioning mechanism. Old plugins break when trait changes.

**Files to modify**:
- `crates/daq-hardware/src/plugin/driver.rs`
- `crates/daq-hardware/src/factory.rs` (registration)
- All driver factory implementations

**Implementation**:
1. Add to DriverFactory trait:
```rust
/// Returns the plugin API version this factory was built against.
/// Current version: 1
fn api_version(&self) -> u32 { 1 }
```

2. In registry registration:
```rust
if factory.api_version() != CURRENT_API_VERSION {
    warn!("Plugin {} built for API v{}, current is v{}",
          factory.name(), factory.api_version(), CURRENT_API_VERSION);
}
```

3. Define version compatibility rules:
   - Same major version: compatible
   - Different major version: warn but attempt load
   - Add `--strict-plugin-version` flag to reject mismatches

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `api_version()` method on DriverFactory
- [ ] Version check at registration time
- [ ] Warning logged for version mismatch
- [ ] `--strict-plugin-version` flag works
- [ ] CLAUDE.md documents versioning policy

**Effort**: 2-3 hours (quick win)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:29:46Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-02-19T20:18:29Z","defer_until":"2026-06-01T00:00:00Z"}

{"id":"bd-izdj.25","title":"[P4] Bluesky-Compatible Metadata Export","description":"**Competitive Context**: Bluesky is the 'gold standard for metadata and reproducibility.' rust-daq should support exporting to Bluesky-compatible formats.

**Problem**: rust-daq stores data in HDF5 but doesn't follow Bluesky's metadata conventions.

   - `/entry/bluesky/descriptors/` - per-stream metadata

4. Add export command: `rust-daq export --format bluesky run_123.h5`

**Metadata Fields to Capture**:

|-------|--------|---------|
| uid | generated | "abc123..." |
| time | system clock | 1706454321.123 |
| plan_name | script name | "wavelength_scan" |
| detectors | device registry | ["prime_bsi"] |
| motors | device registry | ["maitai"] |
| scan_id | incrementing | 42 |

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] HDF5 files include Bluesky-compatible metadata group
- [ ] Export to JSON-L documents (Bluesky format)
- [ ] Can be ingested by Databroker/Tiled (verify with test)
- [ ] Documentation for Bluesky interop

{"id":"bd-izdj.26","title":"[P4] Python Bindings via PyO3","description":"**Competitive Context**: PyMoDAQ, Bluesky, ScopeFoundry all have native Python integration with NumPy/SciPy ecosystem. rust-daq lacks this.

**Problem**: Scientists want to use Python for analysis but rust-daq is Rust-only.

**Files to create** (POST-RENAME CRATE NAME):
- New crate: `crates/python/` (follows rename scheme: no daq- prefix)
- `Cargo.toml` with pyo3 + numpy dependencies
- `src/lib.rs` - Python module definition

**PyPI Package Name**: `rust-daq` (keep brand, distinguishes from crate name)

**Implementation**:
1. Create `python` crate using PyO3
2. Expose key types to Python:
   - `DaqClient` - connect to daemon, control devices
   - `Frame` - zero-copy access to frame data as NumPy array
   - `ParameterValue` - get/set device parameters
3. Use `numpy` crate for zero-copy array sharing
4. Publish to PyPI as `rust-daq` package

**Python API Design**:
```python
import rust_daq

# Connect to daemon
client = rust_daq.connect("localhost:50051")

# List devices
devices = client.list_devices()

# Read camera frame as NumPy array (zero-copy!)
frame = client.read_frame("prime_bsi")
print(frame.data.shape)  # (2048, 2048)
print(frame.data.dtype)  # uint16

# Control device
client.set_parameter("maitai", "wavelength", 800.0)
position = client.get_parameter("maitai", "wavelength")

# Subscribe to stream
for frame in client.stream_frames("prime_bsi", max_fps=30):
    # Process with NumPy/SciPy
    mean = np.mean(frame.data)
    print(f"Frame {frame.number}: mean={mean:.1f}")
```

**Zero-Copy Strategy**:
- Use `numpy::PyArray2` with shared memory backing
- Arrow arrays → NumPy via zero-copy conversion
- gRPC frames → Python with minimal copying

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `pip install rust-daq` works
- [ ] Zero-copy frame access verified (memory doesn't double)
- [ ] All basic operations exposed (list, read, write, stream)
- [ ] Example Jupyter notebook demonstrating integration
- [ ] Performance benchmark: Python wrapper < 5% overhead

**Effort**: 12-16 hours

**Strategic Value**: Opens rust-daq to the massive Python scientific ecosystem. Scientists can use familiar tools for analysis while rust-daq handles acquisition.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:40:40Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-02-19T19:55:28Z","defer_until":"2026-07-01T00:00:00Z"}

{"id":"bd-izdj.27","title":"[P4] Scan Plan DSL (Bluesky-Inspired)","description":"**Competitive Context**: Bluesky's 'plan' abstraction is incredibly powerful for complex, step-based scans. rust-daq's Rhai scripting is lower-level.

**Problem**: Users have to write imperative Rhai scripts. No declarative scan definition.

**Files to modify**:
- `crates/daq-scripting/src/`
- New: `crates/daq-scripting/src/plans/`

**Implementation**:
1. Create declarative scan plan DSL in Rhai:
```rhai
// Declarative 2D scan
let plan = scan_plan()
    .name("wavelength_power_map")
    .motor("maitai", "wavelength", 700..1000, 10)  // outer loop
    .motor("rotator_2", "angle", 0..360, 5)        // inner loop
    .detector("power_meter")
    .detector("prime_bsi", #{exposure: 100})
    .per_point(|| {
        sleep(50);  // settle time
    })
    .on_complete(|data| {
        save_hdf5(data, "scan_001.h5");
    });

run_plan(plan);
```

2. Implement plan executor that handles:
   - Nested loops with checkpointing
   - Automatic metadata collection
   - Pause/resume without data loss
   - Estimated time remaining

3. Built-in plan types:
   - `grid_scan(motors, detectors)` - N-dimensional grid
   - `line_scan(motor, start, stop, steps)` - 1D scan
   - `fly_scan(motor, detectors)` - continuous motion
   - `time_series(detectors, duration, interval)` - fixed-time acquisition

**Plan Metadata (Bluesky-compatible)**:
```json
{
  "plan_name": "grid_scan",
  "plan_type": "generator",
  "plan_args": {"motors": ["maitai"], "num": 30},
  "num_points": 900,
  "num_intervals": 899,
  "hints": {"dimensions": [["wavelength", "angle"]]}
}
```

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Declarative scan builder API in Rhai
- [ ] Built-in plan types (grid, line, fly, time_series)
- [ ] Automatic checkpointing during scans
- [ ] Progress reporting with ETA
- [ ] Bluesky-compatible plan metadata

**Effort**: 12-16 hours

**Strategic Value**: Brings Bluesky's best feature to rust-daq without requiring Python.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:40:40Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-02-19T19:55:28Z","defer_until":"2026-07-01T00:00:00Z"}

{"id":"bd-izdj.28","title":"[P4] Device Configuration Wizard (PyMoDAQ-Inspired)","description":"**Competitive Context**: PyMoDAQ's Dashboard allows drag-and-drop configuration. rust-daq requires editing TOML files.

**Problem**: Initial device setup is too technical for many scientists.

**Files to modify**:
- `crates/daq-egui/src/panels/`

**Implementation**:
1. Create "Add Device" wizard in GUI:
   - Step 1: Select device type (Camera, Motor, Sensor, etc.)
   - Step 2: Auto-detect available ports/addresses
   - Step 3: Configure device-specific parameters
   - Step 4: Test connection
   - Step 5: Save to configuration

2. Port/device auto-detection:

4. Generate TOML config:
```toml
# Auto-generated by Device Wizard
[[devices]]
id = "laser_1"
type = "maitai"
enabled = true

[devices.config]
port = "/dev/serial/by-id/usb-Silicon_Labs_..."
baud_rate = 115200
```

**Wizard UI Flow**:
```
[Select Type] → [Detect Ports] → [Configure] → [Test] → [Save]
    ↓              ↓                ↓           ↓
  Camera        /dev/ttyUSB0     Baud: 115200   ✓ OK
  Motor         /dev/ttyUSB1     Address: 2
  Sensor        (auto-detected)
```

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Wizard accessible from GUI menu
- [ ] Auto-detection for serial ports
- [ ] Live connection test during wizard
- [ ] Generated TOML matches manual config
- [ ] Can edit existing devices via wizard
- [ ] User testing with 3 non-technical users

**Effort**: 10-14 hours

**Strategic Value**: Lowers barrier to entry. Scientists can set up rust-daq without reading TOML documentation.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:41:16Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-02-19T19:55:29Z","defer_until":"2026-07-01T00:00:00Z"}

{"id":"bd-3yb8","title":"EPIC: LIBS Integration into rust-daq","description":"Integrate LIBS (Laser-Induced Breakdown Spectroscopy) hardware and experiments from the Python LIBS codebase into the rust-daq unified framework.

## Overview
Port the LIBS Python codebase to rust-daq, enabling unified instrument control for both RASHG (maitai) and LIBS systems.

## Hardware Inventory (NEW - different from RASHG)
1. **Andor iStar sCMOS Camera** - Gated ICCD with DDG, MCP gain (Windows SDK)
2. **Andor ATSpectrograph** - Grating control, wavelength calibration (Windows SDK)
3. **Dover Motion XYZ Stages** - High-speed 3-axis, Trigger-On-Position (C++ API, Linux support!)
4. **Spirit Laser** - Picosecond laser via CANopen protocol
5. **NI-DAQmx** - Triggering/pulse generation (Windows)

## Key Experiments to Port
- Single-shot/multi-shot spectroscopy
- Step scans (X, Y, Z, 2D grids)
- Trigger-On-Position rapid acquisition
- Delay scans (DDG timing)
- Radiance calibration

## Architecture Notes
- Dover Motion has C++ API with Linux support (no PyO3 needed!)
- Andor SDK3 requires FFI bindings via bindgen
- Spirit laser can use existing Rust CANopen crates
- Feature flag: `libs` for LIBS-specific drivers

## Documentation
- Dover Motion API: LIBS/docs/dover-motion-api/
- Original Python code: LIBS/initialization.py, LIBS/experiments.py","design":".octocode/plan/libs-integration/plan.md","notes":"Reparented 2026-02-10 into phased backlog hierarchy (Phase 4: LIBS Integration Delivery).

{"id":"bd-rgnx.4","title":"Phase 2: Add Frame Pool Timeout Protection","description":"## Objective
Prevent UI stalls from crashing hardware by adding timeouts to frame pool acquisition.

## Current Problem
- FrameProducer uses pool::Loaned<FrameData> for zero-copy streaming
- If UI holds 100% of pool buffers (paused debugger, memory leak, slow processing):
  - Camera driver blocks indefinitely waiting for buffer
  - Hardware loop hangs
  - Potential SDK timeout/crash
  - No visibility into the issue

## Implementation Plan
1. Add timeout parameter to pool buffer acquisition

```rust
        range: 1..=16,
        pvcam_param: PARAM_BINNING_X,
    },
    // ...
}
```

2. Macro generates:

{"id":"bd-rgnx.18","title":"Phase 2: HDF5 Backpressure Strategy","description":"## Objective
Define explicit behavior when HDF5 Actor channel is full.

## Rationale (from review)
This is an ESSENTIAL COMPANION to bd-rgnx.5 (HDF5 Actor). If the HDF5 writer lags behind the camera, the channel fills up. Without a defined strategy, the system will either:
- Block the camera (stalling acquisition)
- Panic (crashing the daemon)
- Silently drop data (data loss)

## Options to Evaluate
1. **Bounded Channel + Block**: Camera blocks until HDF5 catches up
   - Pro: No data loss
   - Con: Camera timing affected, potential SDK timeouts

2. **Bounded Channel + Drop Oldest**: Overwrite oldest frame in channel
   - Pro: Camera never blocks
   - Con: Data loss (but predictable)

3. **Bounded Channel + Drop Newest**: Reject new frame if channel full
   - Pro: Oldest data preserved
   - Con: Loss of most recent data

4. **Adaptive**: Reduce camera frame rate when channel pressure detected
   - Pro: Graceful degradation
   - Con: Complex implementation

## Recommended Approach
Option 2 (Drop Oldest) with metrics and logging:
- Log warning when frames dropped
- Expose metrics: frames_dropped_hdf5_backpressure
- Make channel size configurable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Backpressure strategy defined and documented
- [ ] Channel size configurable
- [ ] Metrics exposed for monitoring
- [ ] Warning logged when frames dropped
- [ ] Integration test validates behavior

## Dependencies
- bd-rgnx.5 (HDF5 Actor Pattern)","notes":"2026-02-08: Tap registry already handles frame-level backpressure. HDF5 writer uses ring buffer with existing drop policy. Remaining concern is explicit HDF5 write-queue overflow policy — deprioritized to P3.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:18:21Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-02-19T20:18:32Z","defer_until":"2026-06-01T00:00:00Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-noqp","title":"Unblock Qwen3.5-397B chat completions for dogfooding","description":"Apply Codex-recommended debugging steps to fix the Qwen3.5-397B chat completions issue where the model produces flat/random logits after chat template special tokens.

Symptom: /v1/completions works perfectly (sharp logits, coherent output). /v1/chat/completions produces garbage or immediate EOS after processing <|im_start|>, <|im_end|>, <think> tokens. CPU-only reproduces it, ruling out GPU/offload issues.

Codex analysis (2026-02-25): Points to chat-path/template-token/tokenizer-metadata interaction, NOT GPU offloading or quantization.

Debugging steps (priority order):
1. A/B test: render prompt with /apply-template, feed exact string to /v1/completions
   - If works: bug is in chat completions server path (message assembly/prefill/reasoning)
   - If fails: bug is in template tokens / tokenizer metadata / GGUF
2. Try all chat server flags together: --reasoning-format none --reasoning-budget 0 --no-prefill-assistant --no-cache-prompt --cache-reuse 0
3. Compare /tokenize output for <|im_start|> (248045), <|im_end|> (248046), <think> against upstream HF tokenizer for Qwen3.5-MoE — check for GGUF tokenizer metadata mismatch
4. Upgrade llama.cpp past b8145:
   - PR #19849 (merged 2026-02-25): hybrid/recurrent server checkpointing fix (n_past vs position)
   - PR #19866 (merged 2026-02-24): Qwen 3.5 multi-GPU graph split ordering
   - PR #19877 (merged 2026-02-25): re-enable multimodal prompt caching
5. Try a different GGUF source for Qwen3.5-397B-A17B (different conversion pipeline)
6. If all else fails: plain-text instruct scaffold avoiding special tokens entirely (System:/User:/Assistant: format via completions endpoint)

Current state:
- vasp-01: Job 1807 running with OLD config (no --jinja, proxy on :8181)
- vasp-02: No SLURM job running
- Chat proxy deployed at /cluster/shared/scripts/llama-cpp/chat-proxy/proxy.py
- Best result so far: 57 tokens with --chat-template-kwargs '{"enable_thinking": false}' (garbled quality)
- Tracked upstream: llama.cpp #19690, #19858

Acceptance: Qwen3.5-397B responds coherently to chat completions requests with >200 token outputs and sharp logits, OR a reliable workaround is documented and integrated into the proxy.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:32:30Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:32:30Z"}

{"notes":"## Session 3 Findings (2026-02-25)

### UD-Q4_K_XL is fundamentally broken
Comprehensive testing on BOTH nodes (vasp-01 and vasp-02) confirms:
- Even simple text continuation ("The history of computing...") now degenerates
- Code completion works only for trivial 9-token outputs (hello world)
- Longer code completion produces deeply nested parentheses (garbage)
- --jinja vs no --jinja makes no difference — model itself is broken
- Model files identical on both nodes (same timestamps, same sizes)
- This is NOT a proxy issue — the raw /v1/completions endpoint produces garbage

### Testing results (all prompts via /v1/completions, no proxy)
| Prompt | Node | --jinja | Tokens | Quality |
|--------|------|---------|--------|---------|
| "The history of computing..." | vasp-02 | on | 128 | "A mechanical calculator" repeated |
| "The history of computing..." | vasp-01 | off | 35 | Partial coherence then repetition |
| `fn main() { println!("Hello` | vasp-01 | off | 9 | Correct (", world!");\n}) |
| is_prime function body | vasp-01 | off | 256 | Deeply nested parentheses |

### Root cause
Unsloth Dynamic 2.0 Q4_K_XL quantization corrupts model weights for this hybrid
Mamba/attention architecture. The dynamic precision upcasting doesn't preserve the
SSM (Mamba) layer computations, causing logit instability that worsens with output length.

### Recovery plan
1. lmstudio-community Q4_K_M download in progress on slurm-ctl (NFS)
   - Standard llama.cpp b8086 quantization (no Unsloth dynamic precision)
   - 241GB, 7 shards, downloading to /cluster/shared/ai/models/lmstudio-Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-GGUF/
   - ETA: ~4 hours from 23:35 EST (02:30-03:30 AM EST)
2. Test Q4_K_M directly from NFS path (no need to copy to /scratch initially)
3. If Q4_K_M works: update MODEL path, may not even need the proxy
4. If Q4_K_M also fails: issue is in llama.cpp qwen35moe arch, not quantization

### Chat proxy v3 status
- Deployed to /cluster/shared/scripts/llama-cpp/chat-proxy/proxy.py
- Uses pure text continuation (no special tokens)
- Missing _format_tool_definitions added
- Docstring updated from v2 to v3
- SLURM script updated: JINJA_MODE env var (default: on, set off to disable)
- start-proxy.sh launcher script added for manual restart"}

### ik_llama.cpp built and tested

Binary at /cluster/shared/llama-cpp/ik-bin/llama-server, wrapper at run-ik-server.sh
(uses conda glibc 2.34 to work on Rocky Linux 8.8).

### ik_llama.cpp --cpu-moe results (GPU attention, CPU MoE)
- Server starts fine, MoE tensors confirmed on CPU
- Throughput: 10.3 tok/s gen, 43.6 tok/s prompt — good speed\!
- Quality: DEGENERATE — same garbage as mainline GPU mode
- Code continuation (is_prime): repetitive "let mut result = true;" — 178 tokens of garbage
- Text continuation: "a for input and (B for input)" — garbled
- Conclusion: --cpu-moe does NOT fix UD-Q4_K_XL quality. GPU attention also broken.

### ik_llama.cpp CPU-only (-ngl 0, CUDA hidden) results
- Throughput: 4.5 tok/s gen, 30 tok/s prompt (faster than mainline CPU-only at 1.9 tok/s)
- Text continuation with ignore_eos (200 tokens): somewhat coherent (history of
  computing, punched cards, Hollerith) but has garbled words and repetitive themes
- Without ignore_eos: 6-10 tokens then EOS (coherent but too short)
- Proxy test (chat completions through proxy): degenerate garbage — "Task:" format
  still triggers degeneration even with pure text continuation

### Definitive conclusion: UD-Q4_K_XL quantization is fundamentally broken
Tested across ALL combinations:
| Backend | GPU Mode | Tok/s | Quality |
|---------|----------|-------|---------|
| Mainline | GPU (-ngl 99) | ~8.4 | Degenerate (issue #19683) |
| Mainline | CPU (-ngl 0, CUDA loaded) | 1.9 | 128 tokens coherent then CRASH |
| Mainline | CPU (-ngl 0, no CUDA) | 2.1 | 26 tokens mediocre |
| ik_llama.cpp | --cpu-moe (-ngl 99) | 10.3 | Degenerate |
| ik_llama.cpp | CPU (-ngl 0, no CUDA) | 4.5 | Mediocre/short |

Acceptance: Qwen3.5-397B responds coherently to chat completions requests with >200 token outputs and sharp logits, OR a reliable workaround is documented and integrated into the proxy.","notes":"## Session 3 Findings (2026-02-25)

### UD-Q4_K_XL is fundamentally broken
Comprehensive testing on BOTH nodes (vasp-01 and vasp-02) confirms:
- Even simple text continuation ("The history of computing...") now degenerates
- Code completion works only for trivial 9-token outputs (hello world)
- Longer code completion produces deeply nested parentheses (garbage)
- --jinja vs no --jinja makes no difference — model itself is broken
- Model files identical on both nodes (same timestamps, same sizes)
- This is NOT a proxy issue — the raw /v1/completions endpoint produces garbage

### Testing results (all prompts via /v1/completions, no proxy)
| Prompt | Node | --jinja | Tokens | Quality |
|--------|------|---------|--------|---------|
| "The history of computing..." | vasp-02 | on | 128 | "A mechanical calculator" repeated |
| "The history of computing..." | vasp-01 | off | 35 | Partial coherence then repetition |
| `fn main() { println!("Hello` | vasp-01 | off | 9 | Correct (", world!");\n}) |
| is_prime function body | vasp-01 | off | 256 | Deeply nested parentheses |

### Root cause
Unsloth Dynamic 2.0 Q4_K_XL quantization corrupts model weights for this hybrid
Mamba/attention architecture. The dynamic precision upcasting doesn't preserve the
SSM (Mamba) layer computations, causing logit instability that worsens with output length.

### Recovery plan
1. lmstudio-community Q4_K_M download in progress on slurm-ctl (NFS)
   - Standard llama.cpp b8086 quantization (no Unsloth dynamic precision)
   - 241GB, 7 shards, downloading to /cluster/shared/ai/models/lmstudio-Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-GGUF/
   - ETA: ~4 hours from 23:35 EST (02:30-03:30 AM EST)
2. Test Q4_K_M directly from NFS path (no need to copy to /scratch initially)
3. If Q4_K_M works: update MODEL path, may not even need the proxy
4. If Q4_K_M also fails: issue is in llama.cpp qwen35moe arch, not quantization

### Chat proxy v3 status
- Deployed to /cluster/shared/scripts/llama-cpp/chat-proxy/proxy.py
- Uses pure text continuation (no special tokens)
- Missing _format_tool_definitions added
- Docstring updated from v2 to v3
- SLURM script updated: JINJA_MODE env var (default: on, set off to disable)
- start-proxy.sh launcher script added for manual restart","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:32:30Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-25T23:57:30Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-1s68","title":"Improve edit_file fuzzy matching for local model whitespace errors","description":"HydraCoder (30B MoE) generates edit_file calls where old_content doesn't exactly match the file text — typically whitespace differences (extra/missing spaces, indentation). The current whitespace-normalized fallback finds a match but often applies a no-op replacement because the new_content also has the same whitespace differences.

Two approaches to fix:
1. **Smarter fuzzy matching**: strip leading/trailing whitespace per line in both old_content and file text, then match. If found, apply new_content preserving the file's original indentation.
2. **Line-number-based edit mode**: add optional start_line/end_line params to edit_file so the model can specify a line range instead of exact text matching. This is more robust when the model has seen truncated file content.

Evidence from dogfooding: 3/6 iterations had edit_file calls that 'succeeded' via whitespace-normalized match but produced no actual file changes. The model understands what to change but can't produce exact text matches from truncated content.

{"id":"beefcake-vjuq","title":"Reduce stochastic tool-call failures in Worker tier prompts","description":"Despite compact prompts (~400 chars user message) and read_file truncation, ~33% of Worker tier iterations still produce text analysis instead of tool calls. This is stochastic and prompt-content-dependent — the same prompt format sometimes triggers tool calls and sometimes doesn't.

Investigation areas:
1. **Temperature tuning**: test temp=0.05-0.1 to escape deterministic bad paths while maintaining reliability (current: 0.0)
2. **System prompt optimization**: the general_coder preamble (~1300 chars) may have content patterns that suppress tool calls. Try a minimal preamble.
3. **Retry on text-only response**: if the agent returns text without tool calls on turn 1, automatically retry the same prompt (stochastic behavior means retries may work)
4. **Qwen3.5-397B comparison**: the larger model may handle tool calling better — test when Q4_K_M download completes

Current reliability: 67% (4/6 iterations make tool calls). Target: >90%.

{"id":"beefcake-96l9","title":"Swarm: NotebookLM query fails silently with empty error message","description":"During swarm startup, the NotebookLM knowledge base query for 'project_brain' role fails with an empty error: 'KB query failed — proceeding without context role=project_brain error=nlm query failed: '. The error message after 'nlm query failed:' is empty, providing no diagnostic information. The nlm CLI likely isn't authenticated or isn't installed. Fix: (1) Log the full stderr/exit code from the nlm subprocess, not just the parsed error. (2) Consider checking nlm availability at startup and logging clearly if it's missing/unauthenticated. File: crates/swarm-agents/src/notebook_bridge.rs. Discovered during swarm validation run.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T14:22:11Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T13:43:14Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b","title":"NS-0: Rust autonomous swarm blueprint implementation (FSM, Deepthink, Agentic Diff, Memory Compaction)","description":"Implement the NEW_SUGGESTIONS architecture blueprint in beefcake-swarm as a staged, testable Rust program: contextual FSM orchestration, JoinSet-based deepthink fan-out/fan-in, unified-diff agentic editing tools, and token-aware memory compaction, with HPC-aware concurrency and rollout safeguards.","acceptance_criteria":"All sub-epics are decomposed with implementation tasks, linked to existing modernization work, and have acceptance-focused deliverables covering code, tests, and rollout evidence.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:23:31Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:23:31Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.1","title":"NS-1: Foundation contracts and orchestration scaffolding","description":"Define shared orchestration contracts, mode boundaries, common data models, error taxonomy, and provider/runtime configuration so the four new architectural capabilities integrate cleanly with existing swarm code paths.","acceptance_criteria":"A documented and tested foundation layer exists that all mode implementations consume without duplicate model wiring or ad hoc error handling.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:24:15Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:24:15Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.1.1","title":"NS-1.1: Audit current swarm execution paths and identify integration seams","description":"Survey existing contextual/deepthink/agentic code paths, model invocation wrappers, and state persistence locations to identify where the NEW_SUGGESTIONS architecture can be integrated with minimal churn.","acceptance_criteria":"A gap matrix maps proposed components to concrete modules/files, highlights conflicting abstractions, and identifies required refactors before implementation begins.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:23Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:23Z","labels":["dogfood","ns-wave0","swarm-queue"]}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.1.2","title":"NS-1.2: Define shared mode runner traits and orchestrator interfaces","description":"Create common traits and boundaries for mode execution (inputs, outputs, lifecycle hooks, telemetry hooks, cancellation, and resume semantics) so Contextual/Deepthink/Agentic components share a stable contract.","acceptance_criteria":"A compile-checked interface layer exists for mode runners and agent factories, with docs/tests that prove each mode can be plugged in without bespoke wiring.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:24Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:24Z","labels":["dogfood","ns-wave0","swarm-queue"]}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.1.3","title":"NS-1.3: Introduce common domain types for artifacts, critiques, strategies, and summaries","description":"Define typed structs/enums for generator outputs, critique verdicts, strategy sets, synthesized results, memory summaries, and orchestration outcomes to eliminate stringly typed handoffs.","acceptance_criteria":"Cross-agent handoffs compile against shared types and parsers/validators exist for every structured payload used by the new modes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:24Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:24Z","labels":["dogfood","ns-wave0","swarm-queue"]}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.1.4","title":"NS-1.4: Implement orchestration error taxonomy and retry classification","description":"Add a unified error hierarchy covering inference failures, tool failures, parse errors, timeout/cancellation, and policy violations with retryability metadata for deterministic control flow.","acceptance_criteria":"Error handling is centralized, typed, and test-covered; callers can distinguish retriable vs terminal failures without string matching.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:24Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:24Z","labels":["dogfood","ns-wave0","swarm-queue"]}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.1.5","title":"NS-1.5: Add provider/model runtime configuration for local vLLM and cloud fallback","description":"Create a configuration surface for selecting generator/critic/judge/summarizer models, local inference endpoints, concurrency caps, and optional cloud fallback while keeping Rig client creation centralized.","acceptance_criteria":"Mode implementations consume configuration via shared factories; local cluster defaults and override paths are documented and validated at startup.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:25Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:25Z","labels":["dogfood","ns-wave0","swarm-queue"]}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.3.1","title":"NS-2.1: Implement SwarmState enum and typed transition payloads","description":"Define the contextual mode state machine with explicit Drafting, Critiquing, Condensing, Done, and Error states carrying only the data required by each transition.","acceptance_criteria":"State definitions compile with exhaustive matching and unit tests verify payload ownership/borrowing patterns for each transition path.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:25Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:25Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.3.2","title":"NS-2.2: Build ContextualOrchestrator agent bundle initialization","description":"Instantiate generator, critique, and memory agents through shared factories with role-specific preambles and deterministic parameter defaults suitable for iterative refinement.","acceptance_criteria":"Contextual mode constructs all required agents from config, supports test doubles/mocks, and stores runtime parameters (max iterations, thresholds) in one validated struct.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:25Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:25Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.3.3","title":"NS-2.3: Implement async FSM run loop with deterministic handoffs","description":"Build the main non-blocking contextual loop that enforces memory limits, invokes agents, records chat history, and transitions states until Done or terminal Error.","acceptance_criteria":"The run loop completes successful and failure flows without panics, maintains ordered history updates, and emits structured transition logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:26Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:26Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.3.4","title":"NS-2.4: Add critique verdict parser and approval contract handling","description":"Implement strict approval detection (exact APPROVED) with resilient parsing/normalization for critique outputs and explicit refinement prompt generation when fixes are requested.","acceptance_criteria":"Critique responses are parsed deterministically, ambiguous outputs are surfaced as typed errors or fallback actions, and tests cover malformed/noncompliant verdicts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:26Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:26Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.3.5","title":"NS-2.5: Implement contextual failure policies and resume support","description":"Add max-iteration thresholds, recoverable error handling, snapshot/restore of FSM state, and replay guards so long-running loops can resume safely after restarts.","acceptance_criteria":"Contextual runs can serialize/restore state without duplicating agent actions, and failure policy tests cover threshold breaches, inference faults, and resume validation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:26Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:26Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.4.1","title":"NS-3.1: Implement strategy generation prompt + structured parsing","description":"Create the strategy generation phase for Deepthink mode with strict JSON/schema parsing and graceful fallback when the model returns invalid structures.","acceptance_criteria":"Deepthink strategy generation yields validated strategy lists or typed fallback behavior, with tests for valid and malformed responses.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:27Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:27Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.4.2","title":"NS-3.2: Implement JoinSet fan-out execution for strategy workers","description":"Spawn parallel sub-agent implementations via Tokio JoinSet, clone agent templates safely, and collect per-strategy outcomes without blocking the runtime thread.","acceptance_criteria":"Strategy workers execute concurrently, return indexed results/errors, and expose cancellation-safe handles with instrumentation for latency and outcome counts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:28Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:28Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.4.3","title":"NS-3.3: Add timeout, cancellation, and panic/error handling for parallel branches","description":"Wrap strategy workers with timeouts and error normalization, handle JoinSet task panics/cancellation, and ensure partial failures do not crash the orchestrator.","acceptance_criteria":"Deepthink branches can fail independently while the orchestrator remains healthy and reports structured branch failure reasons.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:28Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:28Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.4.4","title":"NS-3.4: Implement fan-in collection policy and judge synthesis reducer","description":"Define minimum-success thresholds, result aggregation, and judge-agent synthesis prompts that evaluate parallel implementations and output a final artifact.","acceptance_criteria":"Deepthink reduction rejects empty/insufficient result sets, synthesizes from valid branches, and logs the reduction decision basis.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:28Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:28Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.4.5","title":"NS-3.5: Add hardware-aware concurrency sizing and 3x V100S defaults","description":"Introduce configurable strategy fan-out sizing and queueing behavior tuned for local 3x V100S hardware while preserving safe fallback values for smaller environments.","acceptance_criteria":"Concurrency sizing is configurable, documented, and benchmarked against cluster-aware defaults without hardcoding assumptions throughout the codebase.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:29Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:29Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.5.1","title":"NS-4.1: Implement Rig ApplyDiff tool schema and ToolDefinition","description":"Create the ApplyDiff Rig tool with typed JSON args, stable name/description, and schema fields for target file and unified diff payload content.","acceptance_criteria":"Tool definitions serialize correctly, invalid tool args fail deserialization predictably, and integration points compile with current Rig usage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:30Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:30Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.5.2","title":"NS-4.2: Enforce workspace path validation and traversal protections","description":"Implement canonicalized path checks and base-directory enforcement so agent-issued patch operations cannot escape the workspace or target unsafe paths.","acceptance_criteria":"Path traversal attempts are rejected with typed errors and security tests cover relative traversal, symlink/canonicalization edge cases, and missing-file behavior.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:30Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:30Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.5.3","title":"NS-4.3: Integrate unified diff parse/apply backend behind a normalized adapter","description":"Evaluate and integrate a unified diff backend (e.g., diffy or udiffx) behind an internal adapter trait so patch parsing/apply errors are normalized for agent feedback.","acceptance_criteria":"Patches can be parsed/applied through one internal adapter API, backend-specific errors are normalized, and backend selection is documented with tradeoffs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:31Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:31Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.5.4","title":"NS-4.4: Wire tool failure feedback into agent self-correction loop","description":"Ensure diff-application failures are surfaced back to the agent with actionable error messages and orchestrator policies that permit constrained retry attempts.","acceptance_criteria":"Failed patch attempts generate structured feedback and bounded retries that improve patch success without infinite loops.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:31Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:31Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.5.5","title":"NS-4.5: Harden generator prompts/policies for diff-only editing behavior","description":"Update generator role prompts and mode policies to prefer unified diff tool usage over full-file rewrites, including minimum context-line and formatting constraints.","acceptance_criteria":"Prompt/policy tests and fixtures show the generator chooses the diff tool for patch tasks and emits policy-compliant diff payloads more consistently.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:31Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:31Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.6.1","title":"NS-5.1: Implement MemoryManager abstraction and summarizer-agent wiring","description":"Create a dedicated memory management component that owns token thresholds, preservation window policy, and a summarizer agent used to compress stale conversation history.","acceptance_criteria":"MemoryManager is configurable, injectable into orchestrators, and testable with mock summarizer behavior.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:33Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:33Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.6.2","title":"NS-5.2: Add token-load estimation strategy with pluggable exact tokenizer path","description":"Implement a fast heuristic token estimator and an optional exact tokenizer adapter so compaction triggers can trade precision for cost depending on runtime configuration.","acceptance_criteria":"Token budgeting can run in heuristic or exact mode, produces traceable estimates, and is covered by calibration tests on code-heavy prompts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:33Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:33Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.6.3","title":"NS-5.3: Implement threshold checks and chunked summarization trigger logic","description":"Build the pre-inference interception path that decides when to compact history, preserves immutable/root context, and selects the compressible historical slice.","acceptance_criteria":"Compaction triggers only when thresholds are exceeded and history is compressible; selection invariants are validated in tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:34Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:34Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.6.4","title":"NS-5.4: Implement summary generation payload serialization and history reconstruction","description":"Serialize historical dialogue into a summarizer payload, request a dense summary, and reconstruct compressed history with explicit markers and preserved recent window semantics.","acceptance_criteria":"Reconstruction preserves root/system context and recent messages, inserts a summary marker consistently, and reduces token load measurably without malformed message types.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:34Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:34Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-w70b.6.5","title":"NS-5.5: Add memory compaction correctness, fidelity, and regression tests","description":"Create tests for catastrophic forgetting prevention, summary marker correctness, compaction idempotence, and known pseudocode pitfalls from the blueprint (e.g., incorrect history push/rebuild logic).","acceptance_criteria":"Regression suites catch context-loss bugs and prove compaction preserves critical constraints across repeated compression cycles.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:35Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:35Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-ft94","title":"Add line-range read_file variant for targeted file reading","description":"read_file currently returns the entire file (or truncated first N chars via SWARM_READ_FILE_MAX_CHARS). When a file is larger than the truncation limit (default 6000 chars), the model can only see the top portion and can't edit content later in the file.

Add optional start_line/end_line parameters to read_file so the model can request specific sections. This enables a workflow where the model:
1. Reads the file header/structure (default read)
2. Reads specific functions or sections by line range
3. Edits those sections with accurate old_content

This is especially important for local models with small context windows where full file content would suppress tool calls.

Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/tools/fs_tools.rs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T16:28:17Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T16:28:17Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-s0lh","title":"edit_file old_content drift after fuzzy-matched edits in same iteration","description":"When tool_choice=required forces multiple edit_file calls in one agent iteration, the model references its own previous edit text as old_content for subsequent edits. After the first fuzzy-matched edit rewrites the file (with reindentation), the model's cached version of the code no longer matches the actual file content. Both exact and fuzzy match fail on turns 4-5. Discovered in dogfood run 4. Possible fixes: (1) Include edit_file success feedback that shows the actual written content, (2) After a successful edit, force a read_file on the next turn, (3) Return the relevant portion of the new file content in the edit_file success response so the model has current state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T17:53:51Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T17:53:51Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-lsjm","title":"llama-server HTTP 500 from context overflow after many tool calls","description":"With tool_choice=required and max_turns=8, the accumulated context (system prompt + user message + multiple tool call/result pairs) can exceed llama-server's capacity after 6-7 turns. Observed in dogfood run 4, turn 7: HTTP 500 'Failed to parse input at pos 300'. Possible fixes: (1) Reduce DEFAULT_WORKER_MAX_TURNS to 5, (2) Add context-aware turn limit that tracks approximate token count, (3) Truncate tool results in agent history after each turn. Current: HydraCoder 30B-A3B MoE with 4 slots @ 32K context on vasp-02.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T17:53:52Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T17:53:52Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-vjuq","title":"Reduce stochastic tool-call failures in Worker tier prompts","description":"Despite compact prompts (~400 chars user message) and read_file truncation, ~33% of Worker tier iterations still produce text analysis instead of tool calls. This is stochastic and prompt-content-dependent — the same prompt format sometimes triggers tool calls and sometimes doesn't.

Investigation areas:
1. **Temperature tuning**: test temp=0.05-0.1 to escape deterministic bad paths while maintaining reliability (current: 0.0)

{"id":"beefcake-wh8x","title":"Structured extraction via schemars::JsonSchema for typed LLM outputs","description":"Use Rig's structured extraction (agent.extract()) with schemars::JsonSchema derive to force LLMs to return typed Rust structs instead of parsing free-text responses.

Background: Currently the swarm parses LLM text output manually or uses basic JSON parsing. Rig's extract() method generates a JSON schema from Rust types and constrains the LLM to return conforming output, eliminating parsing failures.

Pattern from rig-examples:
```rust
#[derive(Deserialize, schemars::JsonSchema)]
struct CodeReview {
    severity: String,
    issues: Vec<Issue>,
    suggestion: String,
    confidence: f64,
}

let review: CodeReview = agent.extract("Review this code: ...").await?;
```

Target types for structured extraction:
1. VerifierResult — pass/fail, error categories, fix suggestions
2. WorkPacketAnalysis — task classification, file contexts, key symbols, complexity score
3. CodeReviewOutput — severity, issues list, suggestions, confidence
4. EscalationDecision — should_escalate, reason, recommended_tier, context summary
5. PlannerOutput — steps list, file targets, dependency graph, risk assessment

Implementation:
1. Add schemars dependency to swarm-agents Cargo.toml
2. Define JsonSchema-deriving structs for each target type
3. Replace agent.prompt() with agent.extract() where structured output is needed
4. Add fallback parsing for when extraction fails (model returns non-conforming JSON)
5. Test with both Qwen3.5 (local) and cloud models to verify schema compliance

Acceptance: All 5 target types use structured extraction with >95% parse success rate across model tiers.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:32:05Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:32:05Z"}

This means every feature/fix must be applied twice. Refactor DaqApp to compile to both native and wasm32 using cfg guards for platform-specific features (SSH tunneling, daemon auto-start, file dialogs). new_value:
{"notes":"HARDENING EXECUTION PLAN

Research completed 2026-02-28. The UI crate (`crates/ui/`) contains ~3500 lines in app.rs alone, with 85+ `#[cfg(` annotations across the crate. The codebase already has solid WASM stub infrastructure (panels/mod.rs wasm_stubs, runtime.rs abstraction, time.rs abstraction) but several hardening gaps remain.

## Key Findings

1. **Native-only deps in general `[dependencies]`**: `experiment`, `hardware`, `clap`, `which`, `dirs`, `tracing-subscriber` are NOT behind `cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))` in Cargo.toml. They live in the shared dependency section but are only used by native-only modules. This is the highest-risk item: if any of these crates pull in sys-deps that fail WASM compilation, the web build breaks.

2. **Unguarded `std::process::Command` in shared code**: `app.rs:2976` calls `std::process::Command::new("rerun").spawn()` inside `render_nav()` which is called on both platforms. This will fail or panic on WASM.

3. **Constructor duplication**: `DaqApp::new()` (native, ~300 lines) and `DaqApp::new_wasm()` (WASM, ~130 lines) share ~60% identical initialization logic (theme, shortcuts, style, dock state, device panels, orphan cleanup). This duplication means any bugfix to initialization must be applied twice.

4. **Config-driven panels native-only**: `docked_config_driven_panels`, `config_cache`, `grpc_ui_config_cache` are `#[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]` fields. On WASM, config-driven device panels silently do nothing rather than showing a stub message.

5. **Module structure divergence**: `lib.rs` gates modules by `target_arch` while `main.rs` gates by `feature = "standalone"`. The web feature path (`lib.rs` -> `web_main.rs`) uses a different module gating pattern. This double-gating is correct but fragile.

6. **WASM connection has dead code path**: `wasm_connect()` at app.rs:2711 sets up `connect_rx` channel infrastructure (lines 3173-3191 in update()) but `wasm_connect()` itself does synchronous connection (lines 2721-2726), making the channel polling dead code.

7. **gui_config.rs filesystem access**: `find_gui_config_path()` uses `std::env::current_exe()` and filesystem operations that fail on WASM. The function itself is only called from native paths, but it has no explicit cfg gate.

## Slice 1: Cargo.toml Dependency Isolation
**Scope**: `crates/ui/Cargo.toml`
**Description**: Move `experiment`, `hardware`, `clap`, `which`, `dirs`, `tracing-subscriber` from the shared `[dependencies]` section into `[target.'cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))'.dependencies]`. These crates are only used behind `#[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]` modules. Currently they compile (and link) for WASM even though they are dead code on that target. This is the cheapest fix with the largest blast radius reduction.
**Rollback**: Single-file revert: `git checkout HEAD -- crates/ui/Cargo.toml`
**Verification**:
  - `cargo check -p ui` (native default features)
  - `cargo check -p ui --features web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` (WASM web build)
  - `cargo nextest run -p ui` (all unit tests)

## Slice 2: Platform-guard Shared UI Code (render_nav + gui_config)
**Scope**: `crates/ui/src/app.rs` (render_nav method), `crates/ui/src/gui_config.rs`
**Description**: Add `#[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]` guard around the "Open Rerun" button that calls `std::process::Command::new("rerun").spawn()` at app.rs:2972-2977. On WASM, either hide the button or show a disabled placeholder. Also add explicit cfg gate to `gui_config.rs::find_gui_config_path()` to prevent accidental future calls from WASM paths. These are surgical, bounded fixes to shared code that does not touch constructors or the update loop.
**Rollback**: `git checkout HEAD -- crates/ui/src/app.rs crates/ui/src/gui_config.rs`
**Verification**:
  - `cargo check -p ui` (native)
  - `cargo check -p ui --features web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` (WASM)
  - `cargo nextest run -p ui` (unit tests)
  - Manual: confirm native GUI still shows Rerun button and it works

## Slice 3: Deduplicate Constructor Initialization (new vs new_wasm)
**Scope**: `crates/ui/src/app.rs` (DaqApp::new, DaqApp::new_wasm, plus new helper methods)
**Description**: Extract shared initialization logic from `DaqApp::new()` (lines ~1155-1452) and `DaqApp::new_wasm()` (lines ~1457-1617) into a private helper method (e.g., `DaqApp::init_common(cc, runtime, app_settings, shortcut_manager, theme_preference, control_panel_layout_mode) -> CommonInit`). The helper would handle: font setup, theme loading, shortcut loading, style config, health channel creation, device panel restoration, dock state init, and orphan cleanup. Each platform constructor would call the helper and then set platform-specific fields. This reduces the two ~60%-overlapping constructors to a single source of truth for shared init.
**Rollback**: `git checkout HEAD -- crates/ui/src/app.rs`
**Verification**:
  - `cargo check -p ui` (native)
  - `cargo check -p ui --features web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` (WASM)
  - `cargo nextest run -p ui` (all unit tests including app tests)
  - Manual: confirm both native and WASM apps start correctly with persisted state

## Slice 4: Clean Up WASM Connection Dead Code
**Scope**: `crates/ui/src/app.rs` (WasmConnectionState, wasm_connect method, update polling)
**Description**: The `WasmConnectionState.connect_rx` field and the async polling block in `update()` (lines 3173-3191) are dead code because `wasm_connect()` performs a synchronous connection and never populates `connect_rx`. Either: (a) remove the dead channel and simplify `WasmConnectionState`, or (b) convert `wasm_connect()` to actually use the async channel pattern for proper error handling. Option (b) is preferred because it would enable connection timeout and retry UI for WASM users. This is a WASM-only change that does not touch native code paths.
**Rollback**: `git checkout HEAD -- crates/ui/src/app.rs`
**Verification**:
  - `cargo check -p ui --features web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`
  - `cargo check -p ui` (native should be unaffected)
  - Manual: test WASM connection to a running daemon in browser

## Slice 5: WASM Config-Driven Panel Stubs
**Scope**: `crates/ui/src/panels/mod.rs` (wasm_stubs module), `crates/ui/src/app.rs` (render_device_control)
**Description**: On WASM, config-driven panels (`docked_config_driven_panels`, `config_cache`, `grpc_ui_config_cache`) are completely absent, causing TOML-manifest devices to show as generic panels with no UI schema. Add a WASM stub that parses the `ui_schema_json` from the gRPC DeviceInfo (already available on both platforms) and renders a basic read-only control panel using the existing `web_schema.rs` types. This improves WASM parity without touching native config loading.
**Rollback**: `git checkout HEAD -- crates/ui/src/panels/mod.rs crates/ui/src/app.rs`
**Verification**:
  - `cargo check -p ui --features web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`
  - `cargo nextest run -p ui` (web_schema deserialization tests)
  - Manual: WASM build shows device schema controls for TOML-manifest devices

## Execution Order

Slices are ordered by risk-to-value ratio (safest first):
1. **Slice 1** (Cargo.toml) -- zero code changes, just dep restructuring
2. **Slice 2** (render_nav guard) -- 2 files, surgical, prevents runtime panics
3. **Slice 4** (WASM dead code) -- WASM-only, no native impact
4. **Slice 3** (constructor dedup) -- app.rs refactor, highest code churn but bounded to constructors
5. **Slice 5** (config-driven stubs) -- new feature, lowest priority hardening"}

{"notes":"BEHAVIOR PARITY CONTRACT — DaqApp Native vs WASM

Generated: 2026-02-28
Evidence base: crates/ui/src/{app.rs, lib.rs, main.rs, web_main.rs, runtime.rs, time.rs, settings.rs, panels/mod.rs, panels/scripts.rs, panels/script_editor.rs, panels/signal_plotter.rs, panels/experiment_designer.rs, graph/mod.rs, widgets/status_bar.rs, widgets/offline_notice.rs, client.rs, connection.rs, gui_config.rs, export/, web_schema.rs}

=====================================================================
SECTION 1: PARITY CHECKLIST
=====================================================================

| # | Feature | Native | WASM | Status | Evidence |
|---|---------|--------|------|--------|----------|
| 1 | App boot (entrypoint) | main.rs: CLI args, tokio multi-thread RT, eframe::run_native, GuiLogLayer | web_main.rs: wasm_bindgen(start), spawn_local, eframe::WebRunner, tracing_wasm | N/A | Intentionally different bootstrap paths; both reach DaqApp |
| 2 | DaqApp constructor | DaqApp::new() with DaemonMode, log_receiver, crash detection | DaqApp::new_wasm() — no daemon launching, no session files, no crash detection | N/A | app.rs:1149 vs app.rs:1456 |
| 3 | Dock layout (egui_dock) | Full DockArea with tabs, persistence via eframe::Storage | Same DockArea with tabs, eframe::Storage via web localStorage | PASS | Same DockState<Panel>, same default_dock_state(), same persistence keys |
| 4 | Panel: Nav | Available, includes "Open Rerun" button (spawns process) | Available, "Open Rerun" button renders but std::process::Command::spawn silently fails on WASM | WARN | app.rs:2972-2977 — missing #[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))] guard |
| 5 | Panel: Getting Started | Available | Available | PASS | panels/getting_started.rs — no cfg blocks |
| 6 | Panel: Instruments | Full InstrumentManagerPanel with tree view, device config, pop-out | WASM stub: shows "Hardware configuration is not available in the browser" placeholder | N/A | panels/mod.rs:64-115 wasm_stubs module |
| 7 | Panel: Devices | Available (deprecated, uses gRPC) | Available (same gRPC via gRPC-web) | PASS | panels/devices.rs — no cfg blocks |
| 8 | Panel: Scripts | File upload via rfd::FileDialog, inline editor, execution | Inline editor works; file upload/open/save show "File dialogs are not available in the browser" error | PASS | panels/scripts.rs:277-314, 763-838 — graceful fallback messages |
| 9 | Panel: Script Editor | Open/Save via rfd::FileDialog | Open/Save show "File dialogs are not available in the browser" error | PASS | panels/script_editor.rs:126-138 — graceful fallback |
| 10 | Panel: Scans | Available | Available | PASS | panels/scans.rs — no cfg blocks |
| 11 | Panel: Scan Builder | Available | Available | PASS | panels/scan_builder.rs — no cfg blocks |
| 12 | Panel: Experiment Designer | Full node graph editor with codegen, translation, save/load | WASM stub: shows "The node graph experiment designer requires native execution" placeholder | N/A | panels/mod.rs:117-144 wasm_stubs; graph/mod.rs codegen+translation gated |
| 13 | Panel: Storage | Available | Available | PASS | panels/storage.rs — no cfg blocks |
| 14 | Panel: Run History | Available | Available | PASS | panels/run_history.rs — no cfg blocks |
| 15 | Panel: Run Comparison | Available | Available | PASS | panels/run_comparison.rs — no cfg blocks |
| 16 | Panel: Modules | Available | Available | PASS | panels/modules.rs — no cfg blocks |
| 17 | Panel: Plan Runner | Available | Available | PASS | panels/plan_runner.rs — no cfg blocks |
| 18 | Panel: Document Viewer | Available | Available | PASS | panels/document_viewer.rs — no cfg blocks |
| 19 | Panel: Signal Plotter | CSV export via rfd::FileDialog | CSV export shows "File dialogs are not available in the browser" error | PASS | panels/signal_plotter.rs:593-608 — graceful fallback |
| 20 | Panel: Image Viewer | Available | Available | PASS | panels/image_viewer/ — no cfg blocks |
| 21 | Panel: Logs | Available (receives tracing events via GuiLogLayer channel) | Available (receives tracing events via tracing_wasm to console; no in-app log forwarding channel) | WARN | lib.rs:21-27 gui_log_layer+log_capture native-only; WASM logs go to browser console only |
| 22 | Panel: Comedi | Full ComediPanel with DAQ controls | WASM stub: shows "NI DAQ hardware control requires a Linux host" placeholder | N/A | panels/mod.rs:146-167 wasm_stubs |
| 23 | Panel: DeviceControl (pop-out) | Available via pop-out from InstrumentManagerPanel | Available via pop-out (but InstrumentManagerPanel is stub, so no pop-out source) | N/A | Pop-out only meaningful with real InstrumentManagerPanel |
| 24 | Connection: gRPC | ConnectionManager with exponential backoff, circuit breaker, auto-reconnect | WasmConnectionState: simple connect-once via DaqClient::connect_web() | N/A | app.rs:733-755 WasmConnectionState; connect_web is synchronous, lazy connect |
| 25 | Connection: auto-reconnect | Yes — ConnectionManager handles state machine | No — must manually re-click Connect button | WARN | No auto-reconnect on WASM; acceptable for browser use but worth documenting |
| 26 | Connection: health checks | Periodic health_check() via ConnectionManager | Not implemented on WASM | WARN | app.rs:3197-3201 health checks native-only |
| 27 | Connection: daemon auto-start | DaemonLauncher spawns local daemon process | Not applicable on WASM (browser cannot spawn OS processes) | N/A | app.rs:824-833 native-only |
| 28 | Menu bar | Full native menu bar (File, Connection, View, Tools, Help) with presets, mode switching | Simplified WASM menu bar: title + server URL input + Connect button + status | N/A | app.rs:3213-3249 WASM menu bar; app.rs:1725 native menu bar |
| 29 | Status bar | Full StatusBar with ConnectionState enum (7 states), error count, RTT | Simplified show_simple() with connected/disconnected bool | N/A | widgets/status_bar.rs:138 vs 199 — intentionally different complexity |
| 30 | Settings window | Available on both | Available on both | PASS | settings.rs — no cfg blocks; Ctrl+, shortcut works on both |
| 31 | Calibration (Settings tab) | Shows "coming soon" placeholder | Shows "coming soon" placeholder | PASS | settings.rs:517-528 — identical on both |
| 32 | Theme (light/dark/system) | ThemePreference loaded from storage, apply_theme() | Same ThemePreference + apply_theme() | PASS | Both constructors load theme_preference from eframe::Storage |
| 33 | Keyboard shortcuts | ShortcutManager, cheat sheet (Shift+?) | Same ShortcutManager, same cheat sheet | PASS | Both constructors load shortcut_manager from storage |
| 34 | File dialogs (rfd) | rfd::FileDialog for open/save across Scripts, ScriptEditor, ExperimentDesigner, SignalPlotter | All show "File dialogs are not available in the browser" | PASS | Consistent error message across all 4 panels; rfd dep excluded from web feature |
| 35 | Async runtime | tokio multi-thread (2 workers) | crate::runtime::Runtime wrapping spawn_local (single-threaded browser event loop) | N/A | runtime.rs — intentionally different; !Send futures OK on WASM |
| 36 | Time/Instant | std::time::Instant | web_time::Instant (performance.now()) | PASS | time.rs — transparent swap, same API |
| 37 | Async sleep | tokio::time::sleep | Custom JS setTimeout-based sleep | PASS | runtime.rs:76-90 — transparent swap |
| 38 | Session files (crash recovery) | Write/read session JSON files, detect crashed PIDs | Not applicable (browser has no filesystem for session files) | N/A | app.rs:54-193 all native-only |
| 39 | Version warning banner | Shows "Daemon X != GUI Y" mismatch banner | Not implemented on WASM | WARN | app.rs:1949-1971 native-only; WASM has no version check |
| 40 | Crash recovery banner | Shows "Session restored after unexpected shutdown" | Not applicable on WASM | N/A | app.rs:1927-1947 native-only |
| 41 | Layout persistence | eframe persistence to disk | eframe persistence to web localStorage | PASS | Same keys, same serialization |
| 42 | App save() | Saves dock state, theme, settings, shortcuts, device panels; writes session file | Saves dock state, theme, settings, shortcuts, device panels (no session file) | PASS | app.rs:3392-3432 — cross-platform save with native-only session file |
| 43 | App drop() | Clears session file, stops daemon launcher | Cleans up device panels (no session file or launcher) | N/A | app.rs:3435-3458 |
| 44 | Config-driven panels | ConfigDrivenPanel from TOML manifests + gRPC ui_schema_json | Not available on WASM (config_cache, grpc_ui_config_cache native-only) | N/A | app.rs:864-871 native-only fields |
| 45 | GUI presets (gui.toml) | Loaded from filesystem via gui_config::load_gui_config() | gui.toml lookup skips XDG dirs on WASM | WARN | gui_config.rs:232 skips dirs::config_dir on WASM; presets effectively unavailable |
| 46 | Export (CSV) | File-based export via std::fs::File | Not functional — export_signal_traces uses std::fs::File | WARN | export/signal.rs uses std::fs::File; no WASM fallback (download via blob URL) |
| 47 | Graph codegen | graph_to_rhai_script generates real code | Returns placeholder "// Code generation requires the desktop application" | N/A | graph/mod.rs:20-26 WASM stub |
| 48 | Command widgets | Full execute/poll cycle via gRPC | Same execute/poll cycle via gRPC-web | PASS | CommandWidgetPalette uses runtime.spawn() which is cross-platform |
| 49 | Device list refresh | Via ConnectionManager auto-refresh on connect | Manual — triggered after wasm_connect() | PASS | Both paths call into same gRPC list_devices |
| 50 | Error display | Consistent error/status messages via LoggingPanel + StatusBar | LoggingPanel available; StatusBar simplified; errors shown in panels | PASS | Error messages shown consistently in panel UIs |

=====================================================================
SECTION 2: DEVIATIONS (INTENTIONAL — N/A STATUS)
=====================================================================

1. **Bootstrap path**: Native uses CLI args + tokio RT + eframe::run_native. WASM uses wasm_bindgen(start) + spawn_local + WebRunner. This is fundamental to platform differences.

2. **InstrumentManagerPanel**: Shows WASM stub placeholder. The real panel depends on hardware crate's DeviceConfigCache and native TOML loading. Browser users should use the Devices panel for device interaction via gRPC.

3. **ExperimentDesignerPanel**: Shows WASM stub. Depends on experiment crate (Plan trait, RunEngine) which has native-only dependencies. Graph editing UI still works on WASM but code generation and plan execution do not.

4. **ComediPanel**: Shows WASM stub. NI DAQ hardware control requires Linux + Comedi kernel drivers — fundamentally impossible in a browser.

5. **Connection management**: Native uses ConnectionManager with exponential backoff and circuit breaker. WASM uses simple connect-once model. Acceptable because browser sessions are typically shorter and reconnection can be manual.

6. **Daemon auto-start**: Native can spawn a daemon child process. WASM cannot — browser must connect to a pre-running daemon.

7. **Session files and crash recovery**: Native writes PID-based session files for crash detection. Browser has no equivalent — page reload is the recovery mechanism.

8. **Menu bar complexity**: Native has full File/Connection/View/Tools/Help menus. WASM has a compact connection bar. Appropriate for the browser form factor.

9. **Config-driven panels and GUI presets**: Depend on filesystem access (TOML files, gui.toml). Not available in browser context.

=====================================================================
SECTION 3: ISSUES FOUND (NEED FIXING OR TRACKING)
=====================================================================

1. **[LOW] Nav panel "Open Rerun" button not cfg-gated** (app.rs:2972-2977)
   - `std::process::Command::new("rerun").spawn()` renders on WASM but silently fails
   - Fix: wrap in `#[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]` to hide the button in browser
   - Severity: Low — no crash, just a confusing non-functional button

2. **[LOW] WASM Logs panel has no in-app log forwarding** (lib.rs:21-27)
   - GuiLogLayer and log_capture are native-only modules
   - WASM logs go to browser console (tracing_wasm) but the Logs panel may not receive events
   - Severity: Low — browser console is the primary log destination for WASM

3. **[LOW] No auto-reconnect on WASM** (app.rs:3160-3191)
   - If daemon goes down, WASM user must manually re-click Connect
   - Consider: simple retry logic with exponential backoff in wasm_connect
   - Severity: Low — acceptable for browser use

4. **[LOW] No health checks on WASM** (app.rs:3197-3201)
   - Connection staleness not detected; user sees "Connected" until next RPC fails
   - Consider: periodic health_check() call using wasm spawn + timeout
   - Severity: Low — stale connections surface as RPC errors

5. **[LOW] No version mismatch warning on WASM** (app.rs:1949-1971)
   - render_version_warning is native-only
   - WASM users get no warning if daemon version differs
   - Severity: Low — version mismatches are rare in typical deployments

6. **[LOW] CSV export non-functional on WASM** (export/signal.rs)
   - Uses std::fs::File which is unavailable on WASM
   - Signal plotter shows error message, but export module itself would panic if called
   - Fix: add browser download via Blob URL as a WASM alternative, or keep the error message
   - Severity: Low — error message prevents call path

7. **[INFO] experiment/hardware crates as non-optional deps** (Cargo.toml:89-90)
   - These are in general [dependencies], not behind cfg(not(wasm32))
   - May cause compilation issues if experiment/hardware have non-WASM-compatible transitive deps
   - Currently works because code using them is behind cfg gates, but is fragile
   - Consider: making them optional and including only in standalone feature

=====================================================================
SECTION 4: SUMMARY VERDICT
=====================================================================

The DaqApp native/WASM unification (PR #387) is WELL-IMPLEMENTED. Key findings:

- **18 panels available on both platforms** with identical panel enum and dock layout
- **3 panels intentionally stubbed** (Instruments, Experiment Designer, Comedi) with clear user-facing messages
- **All file dialog uses** gracefully degrade with consistent "not available in browser" messages
- **Runtime abstraction** (runtime.rs, time.rs) cleanly isolates platform differences
- **Connection model** is appropriately simplified for WASM (connect-once vs full state machine)
- **State persistence** uses the same eframe::Storage API on both platforms

No critical or high-severity issues found. 7 low-severity items identified, all with known workarounds or acceptable degraded behavior."}
